programme.desc: A young woman, through sheer force of will, survives a brutal rape. Detectives Stella Bonasera and Danny Messer's mission to find the attacker is hindered, as the victim has no memory of the assault.

programme.desc: A practical joke spirals into a murder mystery when a skeleton placed on a tour bus turns out to be authentic.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The final three bakers have fought their way through 27 baking challenges and now just three challenges lie between them and the trophy. Who will be crowned winner?

programme.title: The Great Kiwi Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on the ultimate challenge during biscuit week, whisking up a storm of delectable treats that crumble expectations and sweeten the competition.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Next Level Chef UK
programme.desc: The chefs must fuse two country's cuisines together and create one incredible dish - but of course they have no idea what country's they'll be cooking with until the platform arrives!

programme.title: Donna Hay: Basics To Brilliance

programme.title: Jamie's 30 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver prepares super-fast beef hash with jacket potatoes, goddess salad and lovely butter beans and bacon.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Foolproof Cooking

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon reworks his favourite classic recipes. Recipes include classic boiled eggs with anchovy toast for breakfast, and eldest daughter Megan helps cook a sensational stuffed rib of roast beef.

programme.title: MasterChef UK: The Professionals
programme.desc: Knockout Week continues. The chefs head to Boxhall in Central London for the MasterChef Pop Up challenge. Then they cook a Family Favourite dish back in the kitchen.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

programme.desc: Jamie celebrates spring picking asparagus wrapped in bacon served with seafood, plants tomatoes, makes spring greens ravioli, spicy green chicken curry & broccoli pizza.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Stories
programme.desc: Rick meets sheep farmer James Rebanks, explores Morecambe Bay for potted shrimps, and cooks his mum's shepherd's pie with son Jack.

programme.title: Food Safari Fire

programme.title: Magical Japanese

programme.title: Easy Japanese
programme.desc: #13 I want to see the snow. / To say what you want to do, replace the "masu" ending of a verb with "tai desu." For example, "mimasu" (to see) becomes "mitai desu" (to want to see).

programme.desc: A small bookstore in Tokyo specializes in Vietnamese books. From the reactions of customers living in a foreign land, we can feel how books in their native language serve as spiritual nourishment.

programme.desc: We visit a store opened by American Kevin Meyerson in Yamanouchi, Nagano. Catering to foreign residents who work in local industries, the shop is a workers' cooperative aimed at enlivening the town.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo Mini
programme.desc: Seri, or Japanese parsley, is in season from winter to spring. It's a key source of nutrition during (Japan's) cold winter months, when most other veggies are out of season.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to the number eight. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Easy Japanese
programme.desc: #13 I want to see the snow. / To say what you want to do, replace the "masu" ending of a verb with "tai desu." For example, "mimasu" (to see) becomes "mitai desu" (to want to see).

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: A small bookstore in Tokyo specializes in Vietnamese books. From the reactions of customers living in a foreign land, we can feel how books in their native language serve as spiritual nourishment.

programme.title: Hello! NHK World Japan
programme.desc: March 2026 marks 15 years since the Great East Japan Earthquake. Throughout the month, NHK World-Japan will show programs looking at the present and future of the disaster-hit areas.

programme.title: A Tale of Two Sisters

programme.title: Noroi: The Curse
programme.desc: A paranormal journalist named Kobayashi goes missing shortly after completing a documentary. What begins as an investigation into strange noises soon evolves into the chilling mystery of a demonic entity.

programme.title: The Hit

programme.title: Quest for Fire

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: In prison, Jamie discovers that an old foe has become the warden and has the power to make his life hell. Claire and Frank both put their best foot forward in marriage.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: While serving as a groomsman at Helwater, Jamie is pulled into the intrigue of a British family. In 1968, Claire, Brianna and Roger struggle to trace Jamie's whereabouts.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Brianna grapples with life-changing revelations and Claire must help her come to terms with the fact that she is her father's daughter. Roger brings news that forces Claire and Brianna to face a choice.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Trusting in Claire's knowledge of 'history', Jamie leads the Jacobite army into a critical battle with British opposition near the town of Preston.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As Jamie puts all of his efforts into turning the Jacobite army away from the impending slaughter at Culloden Moor, Claire attempts to comfort the sick Alex Randall.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Flashing forward to 1968, Claire reveals the truth about her parentage to her daughter. Back in the 18th century, the day of the Battle of Culloden has arrived.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Trusting in Claire's knowledge of 'history', Jamie leads the Jacobite army into a critical battle with British opposition near the town of Preston.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Claire and the Highlanders are sent north after the Jacobite leaders decide to halt their march on London. A band of redcoats makes trouble for the Scots, leading to a most unexpected reunion for Claire.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As Jamie puts all of his efforts into turning the Jacobite army away from the impending slaughter at Culloden Moor, Claire attempts to comfort the sick Alex Randall.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Flashing forward to 1968, Claire reveals the truth about her parentage to her daughter. Back in the 18th century, the day of the Battle of Culloden has arrived.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: After living through the Battle of Culloden, Jamie is at the mercy of British victors, until his past provides his only hope of survival. Meanwhile, Claire attempts to adjust to life in 1940s Boston.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Jamie leads a lonely life until Lallybroch is threatened by redcoats pursing the elusive Jacobite traitor. In Boston, Claire and Frank struggle to co-exist in a marriage haunted by the ghost of Jamie.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: In prison, Jamie discovers that an old foe has become the warden and has the power to make his life hell. Claire and Frank both put their best foot forward in marriage.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: While serving as a groomsman at Helwater, Jamie is pulled into the intrigue of a British family. In 1968, Claire, Brianna and Roger struggle to trace Jamie's whereabouts.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Brianna grapples with life-changing revelations and Claire must help her come to terms with the fact that she is her father's daughter. Roger brings news that forces Claire and Brianna to face a choice.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: After decades apart, Jamie and Claire finally reunite and rekindle their emotional and physical bonds, but Jamie's new business dealings jeopardise the couple's hopes for a simple life together.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Claire follows her conscience as a surgeon, even though it could put her and Jamie's lives at risk. Jamie attempts to evade the reach of the Crown as its representative closes in on his illegal dealings.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Claire returns to Lallybroch with Jamie, where she does not receive quite the reception she was expecting. Unbeknown to her, Jamie's made some choices in their time apart which come back to haunt them.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Claire and Jamie leave Scotland, sailing to the West Indies on an urgent quest. When the superstitious crew looks for someone to blame after a string of bad luck, rescue comes from an unlikely source.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Claire races to discover the source of an epidemic aboard a disease-stricken ship. Jamie locks horns with Captain Raines. Fergus finds himself torn between loyalty and love.

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: After a leap of faith, Claire washes up on an island where survival is her only option. Navigating treacherous waters cripples the Artemis, so Jamie devises a joyful moment for his crew in the midst of setbacks.

programme.title: Outlander

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Eager to please the judges this time are two teams of chefs who work in upmarket patisserie shops, while the third team hails from the kitchens of an exclusive Scottish hotel.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Competing is a cookery school teacher with two of his former star pupils, a trio of pastry chefs from a five-star London hotel, and the only all-French outfit in the competition from a high-end patisserie.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge welcomes a team from a fine-dining restaurant in Liverpool, a team from Leeds led by a chocolatier, and three pastry chefs from south Wales who are representing their country in the culinary Olympics.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for the creme de la creme of British pastry has reached the semifinals. Fifteen teams started the competition, but now just six remain. And things are about to get even tougher.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: It's the second semifinal in the search for the creme de la creme of British pastry. Three teams are back to showcase their high-end skills, hoping to secure their place in the final.

programme.desc: The search for the best team of pastry chefs has reached the final. Fifteen teams started the competition but now just three remain, all hoping to be named the creme de la creme of British pastry.

programme.desc: Hosted by Angus Deayton, the pastry competition returns. Five teams battle it out and are put through two testing challenges in just 36 hours, presided over by returning judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: It is the second heat of the first leg of the competition hosted by Angus Deayton. The teams are tasked with baking 36 uniform eclairs and 36 identical Mont Blanc's and for their show piece, a gateau de mariage.

programme.desc: In the final heat, three teams battle it out for two places in the semifinals. For their miniatures challenge, they face making 72 perfectly baked viennoiseries and 72 tantalising cup petits fours.

programme.desc: Hosted by Angus Deayton, the four remaining teams of pastry chefs once again face the scrutiny of the judges as they attempt to create 36 identical petite Charlottes, and 36 shaped macarons in just two hours.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Hosted by Angus Deayton, the heats draw to a close as two teams battle it out for the last two semifinal places. The judges ask the teams to create 72 designer viennoiserie and 72 exquisite, rolled petits fours.
